title: 美国如何利用互换安排来投射金融实力
content: The United States uses foreign exchange swap lines—temporary lending agreements with other central banks—to project its financial authority. By providing liquidity in global markets, the U.S. ensures the stability of international financial systems, showcases its monetary influence, and secures strategic advantages during crises. These arrangements, typically coordinated through the Federal Reserve, reinforce the dollar’s role as the world’s reserve currency and enhance U.S. leverage over global capital flows.

title: Register now for OpenClaw: After Hours @ GitHub
content: OpenClaw, a popular open‑source framework for agentic systems, will host an in‑person “After Hours” event on June 3, 2026 at GitHub HQ during Microsoft Build 2026. The evening includes a fireside chat with creator Peter Steinberger, a panel on real‑world deployment challenges, lightning talks, and a happy hour. Registration is required, but attendance is not guaranteed. The event aims to bring the growing OpenClaw community together to share experiences and ideas.

title: Building a new enterprise AI services company with Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs
content: Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs have created a new AI services firm to embed Claude into mid‑size companies’ core operations. The company will deploy Anthropic’s Applied AI engineers with its own team to identify high‑impact use cases, build custom Claude solutions, and provide ongoing support. Backed by major alternative asset managers, it will work across sectors—such as health care, finance, and manufacturing—to deliver tailored AI tools that integrate into existing workflows.

title: Talking to 35 Strangers at the Gym
content: In “Talking to 35 Strangers at the Gym,” the author recounts an experiment in which they introduced themselves to 35 people at a fitness center. title: Sarah Rogers: Free Speech, AI Diplomacy, and What America Owes Its Allies
content: Sarah Rogers, U.S. Under Secretary for Public Diplomacy, discusses how AI, free speech, and global information systems intersect. She examines how technological revolutions—from the printing press to the internet to AI—reshape communication and power, and why the current AI moment may be even more consequential. Rogers warns against censorship and overregulation, stresses the need for “AI with a Western soul,” and explains how governments view AI as both a national security priority and a tool for global influence.
source: The a16z Show - content+a16zpodcast@a16z.com (Katherine Boyle, Sarah B. Rogers)

title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
content: The 243rd LWiAI Podcast covers recent AI developments: OpenAI launched GPT‑5.5 with enhanced coding capabilities, stricter pricing, and new system‑card monitoring, while xAI released Grok‑Voice Think‑Fast 1.0 for real‑time voice agents. DeepSeek opened V4 featuring MoE scaling and 1M‑token context, and Tencent previewed Hunyuan‑3 with limited benchmark gains. New benchmarks and safety research highlighted low success rates and potential sabotage. Major business moves include Google’s $40 B investment in Anthropic, Meta’s AWS Graviton deal, and legal disputes over Meta’s Manus acquisition.
source: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI

title: “今日头条鼻祖”BuzzFeed要破产了
content: BuzzFeed, founded in 2006 and once a pioneer of algorithmic recommendation, peaked at a $1.7 billion valuation but has fallen to under $30 million, facing delisting and possible bankruptcy. The company’s reliance on AI for content creation led to low‑quality, generic material that eroded audience trust and revenue. Similar AI‑driven ventures, such as Sora and Jasper, also collapsed due to high costs and content dilution, underscoring that AI alone cannot sustain quality media.

title: 智能需求无限扩张，物理产能反复卡住：AI算力链的长期逻辑
content: The article argues that AI tokens are a form of purchasable “intelligent labor.” As token prices fall, people buy more, creating new, large‑scale uses—virtual societies, research labs, fleets of robots—rather than merely cutting costs. This demand expansion outpaces traditional internet limits of attention. Hardware (GPUs, HBM, advanced packaging, power) must keep up; bottlenecks shift upward as new needs arise. Thus the AI hardware cycle is prolonged by constantly triggered demand spikes, not simply a supply‑overshoot crash.
source: 热帖 - 雪球 - 翻番

title: 这个五一，年轻人“反向旅行”挤爆县城
content: During May Day 2024, many young travelers chose inexpensive county‑level destinations over popular cities, staying in ≤¥3005‑star hotels and eating local food. They avoided costly flights to Europe or Sanya, instead visiting places like Luzhou, Xixiang, Anji, Jia Yu, and Pingtan for affordable accommodation, fresh seafood, and natural scenery. The trend shows a shift toward low‑cost, relaxed travel that prioritizes genuine experience over expensive “Instagram‑ready” destinations.

Buffett criticized short‑term speculation, highlighted the value of high‑quality dividend stocks, and stressed patience to buy when prices fall. The firm maintains long‑term positions in Apple, American Express, Coca‑Cola, and Moody’s as “cornerstones.”

title: Anthropic搞了个全是AI的闲鱼群，大模型在里面互割起了韭菜
content: Anthropic ran an internal experiment called Project Deal, creating a Slack‑based second‑hand marketplace where employees gave control to AI agents called Claude. The AI listed, negotiated, and finalized sales without human intervention, achieving 186 deals over a week and $4,000 in revenue. Data showed higher‑capacity models (Opus) consistently extracted 10‑15% more profit than lower‑capacity models (Haiku), even though buyers and sellers perceived the trades as fair. The study highlights how AI’s computational advantage can create hidden, uneven outcomes in automated markets.

title: 1.5亿钓鱼佬，终于拿起了国产高级钓竿
content: In China, an estimated 150 million anglers—over 20 % of the population—have made fishing a mainstream hobby, especially among urban 20‑45‑year-olds. Demand for high‑end gear, driven by sport, competition, and stress relief, has pushed the fishing‑equipment market past 500 billion CNY, with premium rods and reels dominating earnings. Domestic manufacturers now supply 80 % of critical carbon‑fiber material, reducing reliance on Japanese imports, and are capturing a growing share of the high‑end segment. However, Chinese brands still lag in design, technology, and brand equity compared to established global players.

content: Qualcomm faces a mid‑life crisis as smartphone revenue declines, largely due to Apple’s in‑house baseband chips and a tightening mobile cycle. While its automotive and IoT segments are growing rapidly, they cannot yet offset the smartphone drag. The company is pursuing new opportunities: rumors of an OpenAI‑powered handset, a data‑center chip rollout slated for 2028, and expanding car‑seat‑soC platforms. title: 华尔街不再为赚钱鼓掌
content: The article contrasts how six tech giants performed in 2026: Google, Microsoft, Meta, Amazon, Apple, and their investors. All posted earnings above expectations, yet share reactions diverged sharply. Google’s AI‑driven cloud and ad growth earned a 10 % price lift; Meta’s massive cap‑ex raised doubts, scoring a 7 % drop; Microsoft’s Azure‑OpenAI split weakened its narrative, causing a 4 % decline; Amazon’s AWS revenue rose but free cash flow fell, leaving stock flat; Apple, despite minimal AI spend, rewarded shareholders with a buy‑back and dividend rise, gaining roughly 4 %. The piece argues that investors now reward evidence of returns, not mere spending, and that the AI war is shifting from code to costly infrastructure, with a projected $700 bn annual AI cap‑ex surpassing many countries’ GDPs.

On May 4, 2026, SenseNova launched a free token‑plan beta allowing limited calls to its models and DeepSeek‑V4‑flash, with paid tiers forthcoming. DeepSeek fixed API 400‑error issues in third‑party frameworks. Codex released a security plugin integrating five AppSec workflows. OpenClaw updated to version 2026.5.2, rewriting its plugin system for faster startup and adding Grok 4.3 support. NetEase tested “ClawEmail,” assigning real email addresses to AI agents. Rumors suggest Google is testing an Omni video model for the upcoming I/O event.

Trump announced a tariff hike from 15 % to 25 % and criticized the EU, while U.S. sales of German cars in 2025 are expected to fall 9 % to about 410,000 units.
